title: resolveFabricLocale helper + serve-lock switch + cli i18n γ-pattern reshape
description: Introduce `resolveFabricLocale(projectRoot: string): Locale` in shared i18n package. Resolution order: read .fabric/fabric-config.json::fabric_language → if value is 'en' or 'zh-CN' return it → if 'match-existing' (should never appear post-init per KT-DEC-9004) fall through with console.warn → fall back to detectNodeLocale() → final fallback 'en'. Switch `serve-lock.ts` and reshape `cli/src/i18n.ts` to γ-pattern: keep module-level `t` based on `detectNodeLocale()` for non-projectRoot callers (banner/version/help — existing 6 consumers untouched), AND expose new `getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot: string): Translator` factory used only by doctor command. Add unit test for resolveFabricLocale covering all 4 resolution branches.

files.change: Export `resolveFabricLocale(projectRoot: string): Locale`. Implementation: read `path.join(projectRoot, '.fabric/fabric-config.json')` via fs.readFileSync; parse JSON; if missing/unreadable/invalid-json return detectNodeLocale(); inspect `parsed.fabric_language`; if 'en' or 'zh-CN' return it; if 'match-existing' or 'zh-CN-hybrid' fall through with console.warn (eager-resolve invariant per KT-DEC-9004); otherwise return detectNodeLocale(); final fallback 'en' (handled by detectNodeLocale).

5 test cases: (1) projectRoot with fabric_language='zh-CN' returns 'zh-CN'; (2) projectRoot with fabric_language='en' returns 'en'; (3) projectRoot with fabric_language='match-existing' warns + falls to detectNodeLocale; (4) projectRoot without fabric-config.json returns detectNodeLocale; (5) projectRoot with malformed JSON returns detectNodeLocale + no throw. Use tmp dir fixture via os.tmpdir + fs.mkdtempSync; stub detectNodeLocale via vi.mock if non-deterministic in CI.
Add `export * from "./resolve-fabric-locale.js";` adjacent to existing `export * from "./create-translator.js";` to surface the new helper through the @fenglimg/fabric-shared barrel.

Change `import { createTranslator, detectNodeLocale } from "@fenglimg/fabric-shared";` to `import { createTranslator, resolveFabricLocale } from "@fenglimg/fabric-shared";`. Change `const t = createTranslator(detectNodeLocale());` to `const t = createTranslator(resolveFabricLocale(projectRoot));` — projectRoot is already passed to serve-lock functions (verify by reading the file).

Keep existing `export const locale = detectNodeLocale();` and `export const t = createTranslator(locale);` lines untouched (γ pattern — preserve all 6 existing consumers: hooks-orchestrator.ts, commands/doctor.ts, commands/install.ts, commands/uninstall.ts, commands/serve.ts, commands/config.ts, index.ts). Append: `import { resolveFabricLocale } from "@fenglimg/fabric-shared"; export function getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot: string): Translator { return createTranslator(resolveFabricLocale(projectRoot)); }`. Import Translator type from @fenglimg/fabric-shared as well.

implementation: Read packages/shared/src/i18n/types.ts and create-translator.ts to confirm Locale type shape and createTranslator signature.
Read packages/server/src/services/serve-lock.ts in full to confirm projectRoot is already in scope where t() is constructed.
Read packages/cli/src/i18n.ts (4 lines) — verify all 6 consumers of `t` and `locale` exports — confirm γ pattern leaves them untouched.

Create packages/shared/src/i18n/resolve-fabric-locale.ts. Use a try/catch wrapping fs.readFileSync + JSON.parse; return detectNodeLocale() on ANY failure. Match the explicit fall-through narrative: explicit-en/zh-CN → match-existing-warn → env → 'en'.

Create packages/shared/src/i18n/resolve-fabric-locale.test.ts. Use vitest + fs.mkdtempSync(os.tmpdir() + '/fabric-locale-')+fs.writeFileSync. Don't mock detectNodeLocale — let it return whatever CI gives; assert RELATIVELY (i.e. case 4 returns whatever detectNodeLocale returned, not a hardcoded value).

Edit serve-lock.ts: replace detectNodeLocale with resolveFabricLocale(projectRoot). If projectRoot is NOT in scope at module-load translator construction, lift translator construction into the function that knows projectRoot (likely the lock-acquire/release entry point).
Edit cli/src/i18n.ts: keep existing exports verbatim, add getDoctorTranslator factory below.
Run pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test → green (no regression on 6 existing consumers of t).
Run pnpm typecheck + pnpm lint → green.
Commit: `feat(rc26): resolveFabricLocale helper + γ-pattern doctor translator (TASK-01)`

reference.pattern: Mirrors existing detectNodeLocale + createTranslator usage in serve-lock.ts. γ-pattern factory style precedented by getOnboardSlots(projectRoot) and other projectRoot-aware helpers in server services.

rationale.chosen_approach: γ-pattern (keep module t for non-projectRoot, NEW factory for doctor) over α (lazy module init using process.cwd) or β (force all 6 callers to factory). γ minimizes blast radius — doctor delivers the user pain fix, other commands stay unchanged.
alternatives_considered: α: lazy-init `t` at first call using process.cwd — rejected: implicit projectRoot is fragile, tests pollute global state
β: reshape `t` to factory, migrate all 6 consumers — rejected: scope creep, no user-facing benefit on install/config/serve
decision_factors: KT-DEC-9004 designates fabric_language as authoritative for projectRoot-aware contexts
Existing detectNodeLocale callers are mostly imperative I/O where env-locale is acceptable
Doctor is uniquely a structured diagnostic command where user-config-driven locale matters
rationale.tradeoffs: Inconsistency: doctor respects fabric_language, install/config/serve/uninstall don't. Documented in plan.json out_of_scope and addressed in future RC if user requests.

manual_checks: With pcf .fabric/fabric-config.json::fabric_language='zh-CN', getDoctorTranslator(repoRoot)('cli.doctor.description') returns Chinese version (assuming key exists)
With temp dir lacking fabric-config.json, getDoctorTranslator returns translator using detectNodeLocale env value
success_metrics: cli i18n module still exports `t` and `locale` (verify no breaking change for 6 existing consumers)

criteria: pnpm typecheck exits 0 ; pnpm lint exits 0 ; pnpm test exits 0
Commit msg: 'feat(rc26): resolveFabricLocale helper + γ-pattern doctor translator (TASK-01)'
